creator: de0u@andrew.cmu.edu
text:
Recently I read this amusing/scary/sad piece in Ars Technica:

  Secure Boot is completely broken on 200+ models from 5 big device makers
  Dan Goodin
  Ars Technica
  July 25, 2024
  https://arstechnica.com/security/2024/07/secure-boot-is-completely-compromised-on-200-models-from-5-big-device-makers

Upon reflection, one thing that would have saved them would have been if the their release processes had included an automated check for "DO NOT SHIP" or "DO NOT TRUST" or maybe even "DO NOT ".

With respect to this issue, which is admittedly much less dire, I still think the situation would be improved if the release process for FreeBSD included an automated check that the release signing key is present in a keyring which is present in the documentation.
